item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Imagine if the Masquerade is broken, not because of disasters, but by Amazon Inc.,and their buyouts.

Take my +1, and may SCP!Amazon go bankrupt one day.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Imagine how Alibaba's thriving if Amazon is doing stuff like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The idea's interesting, but I feel like there are a few assumptions that are out of place in the article. For example, the connection between SCP-3914 and "artifacts/objects". I had to re-read the article to figure out that Rubinstein is the reason that the foundation is investigating for anomalous objects from SCP-3914. It's reasonable that one or more of the members with unidentified anomalies can also create anomalous objects, but it's not very clear. I think the best way to combat this would be to put a little bit less emphasis on the what and more emphasis on the why. It's just a minor fix, so I'll give this one an upvote. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Its been awhile since a scip has seriously creeped me out. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
fuckin bezos (+1)

that said, I do have a bit of feedback: I find it odd that the foundation is merely "looking for a way to eliminate SCP-3914". Necromancers or no, at the end of the day it's a handful of old people with some magic artifacts. There's like a zillion things that can instantly obliterate mere humans under Foundation control. I find it hard to believe they couldn't decapitate a dozen average humans in a day.

I kinda just forced myself to suspend disbelief on that because I loved the rest of the concept so much. I'd just rather there be a clearer reason why they haven't been able to eliminate the amazonians yet.

item.title: Re: Necrocapitalism
item.content:encoded: Very nice. I like the idea that a bunch of Necromancers are running Amazon. I don’t necessarily agree with the sentiment echoed on this forum that scps absolutely must be non-people that are tied to real life, and I’m pretty sure there are a lot of quality articles which showcase this. Good work. An article like this on this site nowadays is like a diamond in the rough, and you struck gold with your first scp.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I understand where you'd coming from, but I'd offer that that kind of reaction is exactly why, in-universe, the board would want a puppet like Bezos, a figurehead to soak up blame for the decisions they make. Hell, even out of universe, it's pretty likely that these directors have had a say over some of the policies that Bezos gets all the blame for.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bezos' puppeteers aren't eldritch alien intelligences or prehistoric deities or anything though - they're ordinary greedy humans, just with some magic powers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That is a very strange place to put that information.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: -A and -C are described in the anomalous abilities section of the table; -A instances are sentient but under the control of their creator, while -C are basically ghosts created by -3.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly can't get behind anything that makes Bezos out to be anything but the heartless monster he is. He's not some necrominion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not a big fan of skips that deal with real, living people.

That said, you designate -A, -B and -C instances, but only -B is ever explained. What's the distinction?

item.title: Necrocapitalism
item.content:encoded:
This is my first skip— it started off as just a regular ol' haunted Amazon Echo until someone pointed out the cabal of Necromancers apparently running Amazon was a much more interesting concept.

I've got a lot of crit in the IRC from a lot of different people, and I can't remember all the names exactly. Still, thanks to all of them, and I hope you like the final result.
